name: Semper Fortis
description: Starfleet is forced to turn to those at the top of the chain of command when several retired vessels go rogue. Many feel reinvigorated to be back out amongst the stars, helming a ship once more, rather than behind a desk. But at what cost?
rules:
-- There are two Phases to this Event! Each Phase is individually timed.
-- In this Hybrid Event, you will engage in Ship Battles in one phase, and Specialist Missions in another phase.
-- In a Specialist Event, you will select crew to engage in Specialist Missions.
-- Only a single crew with corresponding skills can be assigned to a Specialist Mission. After the mission is complete, that crew will be disabled from doing Specialist Missions for some period of time.
-- Specialist Missions take time to complete. The higher selected crew skills are the less time it will take them to complete a mission.
-- Only the highest skill of a crew is considered when calculating time to complete a mission.
-- Using crew with matching Specialist Mission bonus traits will significantly decrease time needed to complete a mission.
-- Each time a Specialist Mission is completed, some progress is also gained on the Main Mission. Completing the Main Mission rewards substantial amounts of Event Victory Points.
-- When you fail to gain bonus progress on the Main Mission using featured crew or crew with matching event bonus traits, the bonus progress chance goes up slightly the next time you use such crew in a mission. If you score the bonus progress, the bonus chance amount goes back to its initial value. 
-- In a Skirmish Event, you will engage in space battles against thematically appropriate foes until the Event's conclusion.
-- Ships of any rarity can be used at all difficulty levels.
-- Elite difficulty opponents are upgraded to Epic difficulty level, Elite difficulty VP rewards are upgraded to Epic difficulty VP rewards.
-- Epic difficulty opponent stats are 20% higher, Epic difficulty VP rewards are doubled.
-- Using any version of Hanson, Buenamigo, O'Connor, or Cosmic crew will provide a bonus to Ship Battles and Specialist Missions.
-- Using the U.S.S. Constellation NCC-1017, the U.S.S. Enterprise NCC-1701-F, or the Alternate Probability Cerritos will provide a bonus to hull.
-- Every Ship Battle or Specialist Mission you complete earns you Event Victory Points. Collect Event Victory Points to unlock Threshold Rewards!
-- Community Rewards are also available during Phase 2 only. Collect Event Victory Points to unlock these rewards before the phase change!
-- In this event, Squadrons may also compete to advance up the Squadron leaderboards. Join a Squadron to get more rewards!
bonus_text: Crew Bonus: Hanson, Buenamigo, O'Connor, Cosmic
rewards_teaser: Reach Rank 1 by gaining Victory Points from Event Missions!

featured_crew.name: Billups
featured_crew.flavor: Andy Billups gave up his royal heritage to join Starfleet, where he rose to the rank of Chief Engineer.

action.name: I'm Perfectly Happy Being An Engineer

featured_crew.name: Buenamigo
featured_crew.flavor: Having become an admiral, Buenamigo began conducting experiments, including on Rutherford, without caring about the moral side.

action.name: Once You're An Admiral, You Hit A Wall

featured_crew.name: O'Connor
featured_crew.flavor: O'Connor himself no longer believed that he would succeed in ascending and confessed this to Tendi when the ascension finally occurred.

action.name: I'm Gonna Do It! I'm Gonna Ascend!

rewards.flavor: Chronitons allow your crew to take action in these chaotic times. Use Chronitons to replay parts of past missions via the Timeline.

rewards.flavor: ISM, or interstellar medium, is the matter that exists between astronomical objects. It can be manipulated by the Q to facilitate crew retrieval. ISM can be obtained from Daily Log-in Rewards, Campaigns and participating in Fleet activities.

rewards.name: Dilithium
rewards.flavor: Dilithium is your most valuable asset. Use Dilithium in the Time Portal to acquire valuable crew members, items, and more.

rewards.name: Honor
rewards.flavor: Honor is gained by dismissing crew who have served you well, but are ready for reassignment. Redeem in the Time Portal for exclusive crew, special fusion-related items, training programs, and more.

rewards.name: Merits
rewards.flavor: Merits can be earned by completing missions. Use Merits to acquire faction-based rewards, upgrades to your ship, and more.

rewards.name: Training
rewards.flavor: Increases crew XP by 2500.

rewards.name: Billups
rewards.flavor: Andy Billups gave up his royal heritage to join Starfleet, where he rose to the rank of Chief Engineer.

action.name: I'm Perfectly Happy Being An Engineer

rewards.name: O'Connor
rewards.flavor: O'Connor himself no longer believed that he would succeed in ascending and confessed this to Tendi when the ascension finally occurred.

action.name: I'm Gonna Do It! I'm Gonna Ascend!

content.reroll_info:
Skirmish rewards are additive. By continuously rolling you receive additional rewards. No rewards are lost by rolling again.
DROP RATES:
Event VP: 13.58%
Time Portal Pack: 0.13%
Chronitons: 11.45%
Honor: 14.11%
Intel: 27.43%
Credits: 33.29%

Skirmish rewards are additive. By continuously rolling you receive additional rewards. No rewards are lost by rolling again.

Time Portal Pack: 0.13%

Intel: 27.43%
Credits: 33.29%

rewards.name: O'Connor
rewards.flavor: O'Connor himself no longer believed that he would succeed in ascending and confessed this to Tendi when the ascension finally occurred.

action.name: I'm Gonna Do It! I'm Gonna Ascend!

rewards.name: Buenamigo
rewards.flavor: Having become an admiral, Buenamigo began conducting experiments, including on Rutherford, without caring about the moral side.

action.name: Once You're An Admiral, You Hit A Wall

rewards.name: 10x Portal
rewards.flavor: 10 valuable items. Go to Time Portal to collect!

screens.text: "We've been experiencing an increase of attacks on Federation outposts in the outer reaches of the Alpha Quadrant."
"What concerns me even more is that some of the attacking ships are retired Federation vessels. They should be spacedocked or back over San Francisco, not turning on their own."
"Do you really think a ship knows or cares the affiliation of its target? What was programmed to destroy must destroy, it's as simple as that."
"It thus falls to those of us in command to determine how best to handle this situation. We are counting on your courage, Captain!"
"We've been experiencing an increase of attacks on Federation outposts in the outer reaches of the Alpha Quadrant."
"What concerns me even more is that some of the attacking ships are retired Federation vessels. They should be spacedocked or back over San Francisco, not turning on their own."
"Do you really think a ship knows or cares the affiliation of its target? What was programmed to destroy must destroy, it's as simple as that."
"It thus falls to those of us in command to determine how best to handle this situation. We are counting on your courage, Captain!"
